ab: cikuy samiy raral ga maki Balung ngasal myan, mqwas samiy biru ru, mwah samiy maki Cyama la, yasa si myan aki hogan mqyanux.
ch: 小時候我們家住在巴陵，上學以後，就搬到了大溪，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Balung. After I started school, we moved to Daxi and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: stazil saku mqwas Raygak lga, gmoyaw saku qutux pqbaqun gaga qnxan tayal, kahul sqaniy baq saku tayal Taywan ga bhuyun balay gaga qnxan nha.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: nanu yasa qu, hngawan ’bagan qaniy ga,samiy ka qbsuyan muw musa myan qalang maqut bnkis,sulu’ samiy qzinah baq squ gaga qnxan tayal.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: hminas spat pgan kawas nya yutas qasa, ana ga yan aring mt’ru’ ktan.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: pqutan muw hiya nanu llamu ke baqun su kmal, hiya ga blaq balay ke nya tayal, ru baq kmal ke thiluw kmukan Gipun ana ke Amirika baqun nya uziy.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的泰雅語說得很流利，也會說華語、台語、日語甚至美語也會說。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Atayal, as well as Chinese, Hokkien, Japanese and English.

ab: pqutan maku ru ubaq, cipoq raral ga minnaki qalang tayal ngasal nha, hiya ga kmukan kneril nya ga Gipun, pcyogan nya ga cyux mimaw ka squliq Amirika, misa balay baq piyux llamu na ke.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就住在泰雅族部落，自己是台灣人太太是日本人，而他工作業務上跟美國人有關聯，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he lived in the Atayal when he was a child, his wife is an Atayal, and he knows many American at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: trang samiy mcisal ga, piyux balay gaga na mlata wal nya skayal yutas qasa.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: ana myan ini baqiy iyal mung ga, ana ga wal myan spowah Rinwasrasun psyasing, baqun kbzih mita loziy.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: laqi saku raral ga ini maku baqiy gaga na ita tayal, sksayux maku nanak kuzing ga laqi tayal.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: piyux baqun maku lga, kuzing ka tayal qaniy ga, ubaq si p’anak ru stgsyaw maku sinrxan muw.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為泰雅族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Atayal is unique and something to be proud of.
